channel.title: blog on Gowtham's blog
channel.description: Recent content in blog on Gowtham's blog

item.title: Bitten by Golang Gotcha
item.description: At work, both my previous project and the current project I’m working on relies on SQS for failure retries with exponential backoff. The current project is a webhook service that delivers webhooks to our customers from our products and also among internal services. We use SQS for failure retries and the current queue setup looks like this: queue-name delay offset-delay (hh:mm:ss) visibility-timeout max-receive-count failed-0 30s 00:00:30 10m 10 failed-1 1m 00:01:30 10m 10 failed-2 2m 00:03:30 10m 10 failed-4 4m 00:07:30 10m 10 failed-8 8m 00:15:30 10m 10 failed-15 15m 00:30:30 10m 10 “visibility timeout” above refers to the maximum time SQS will wait before sending the same message to another consumer API call if the message is not deleted by your consumer.
